oils/array-assoc.test.sh::bash variable.reason: gbash cannot represent bash's mixed scalar-plus-associative variables

oils/array-compat.test.sh::value.BashArr.reason: gbash's declare -a listing output still differs from bash's indexed-array introspection

oils/array-literal.test.sh::Tilde expans.reason: gbash still expands ~ in keyed indexed-array literals using the temporary HOME binding unlike bash
gbash still expands ~ in keyed associative-array literals using the temporary HOME binding unlike bash

oils/array-sparse.test.sh::${@:offset:le.reason: the pinned harness still hits a sed incompatibility before bash and gbash results can be compared

oils/array-sparse.test.sh::${a[@]/pat/re.reason: gbash still handles anchored sparse-array replacements differently from bash

oils/array.test.sh::${mystr[@]} and ${my.reason: gbash still treats string scalars as arrays in strict_array parameter expansions

oils/array.test.sh::Arrays can't be used.reason: gbash rejects array-valued env bindings instead of decaying them to strings like bash

oils/array.test.sh::Associative arrays c.reason: gbash rejects associative-array env bindings instead of stringifying them like bash

oils/array.test.sh::Exporting array does.reason: gbash exports array variables differently from bash's no-op behavior

oils/array.test.sh::Length op, index op,.reason: gbash emits a custom parameter-expansion error instead of bash's bad substitution

oils/array.test.sh::Multiple subscripts.reason: gbash emits a custom parse error instead of bash's bad substitution for chained subscripts

oils/array.test.sh::Negative slice lengt.reason: gbash still accepts negative array slice lengths that bash rejects

oils/array.test.sh::Set array item to ar.reason: gbash treats nested array assignment as fatal instead of bash's non-fatal member-assignment error

oils/array.test.sh::Using an array itsel.reason: gbash still coerces array lvalue subscripts differently from bash
gbash's unsupported strict_arith shopt diagnostic text still differs from bash in this array test

oils/array.test.sh::array with invalid t.reason: gbash stops invalid array literals earlier than bash's parser

oils/array.test.sh::declare -a / local -.reason: gbash still differs from bash when declaring empty arrays with declare -a or local -a

oils/array.test.sh::strict_array prevent.reason: gbash still follows bash's export behavior instead of rejecting array export under strict_array
